sea","europe","asia","rivers","coastline"],"similarityValue":0,"id":2,"type":"tools","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Call tools to help you with the reasoning process."},{"role":"system","content":"Tool wikipedia_query_run with arguments {\"query\":\"Depth of the Black Sea\"} gave result: Page: Black Sea\nSummary: The Black Sea is a marginal mediterranean sea lying between Europe and Asia, east of the Balkans, south of the East European Plain, west of the Caucasus, and north of Anatolia. It is bounded by Bulgaria, Georgia, Romania, Russia, Turkey, and Ukraine. The Black Sea is supplied by major rivers, principally the Danube, Dnieper and Dniester. Consequently, while six countries have a coastline on the sea, its drainage basin includes parts of 24 countries in Europe.\nThe Black Sea covers 436,400 km2 (168,500 sq mi) (not including the Sea of Azov), has a maximum depth of 2,212 m (7,257 ft), and a volume of 547,000 km3 (131,000 cu mi). \nMost of its coasts ascend rapidly.\nThese rises are the Pontic Mountains to the south, bar the southwest-facing peninsulas, the Caucasus Mountains to the east, and the Crimean Mountains to the mid-north.\nIn the west, the coast is generally small floodplains below foothills such as the Strandzha; Cape Emine, a dwindling of the east end of the Balkan Mountains; and the Dobruja Plateau considerably farther north.\nThe longest east–west extent is about 1,175 km (730 mi).\nImportant cities along the coast include (clockwise from the Bosporus) Burgas, Varna, Constanța, Odesa, Sevastopol, Novorossiysk, Sochi,  Poti, Batumi, Trabzon and Samsun.\nThe Black Sea has a positive water balance, with an annual net outflow of 300 km3 (72 cu mi) per year through the Bosporus and the Dardanelles into the Aegean Sea. While the net flow of water through the Bosporus and Dardanelles (known collectively as the Turkish Straits) is out of the Black Sea, water generally flows in both directions simultaneously: Denser, more saline water from the Aegean flows into the Black Sea underneath the less dense, fresher water that flows out of the Black Sea. This creates a significant and permanent layer of deep water that does not drain or mix and is therefore anoxic. This anoxic layer is responsible for the preservation of ancient shipwrecks which have be."}],"parents":[1],"children":[3],"textembedding":[-0.10003668,-0.000013511127,-0.016555602,-0.06782942,0.024121962,-0.043021128,0.0460008,-0.034684736,-0.07807414,-0.023435634,0.03193942,-0.014379435,0.016170587,-0.005762655,-0.046335597,0.046134718,0.039505783,-0.0833639,0.058522124,-0.037563972,-0.0019711042,0.16177283,-0.08543962,0.05825429,-0.07713672,0.034919094,0.09099722,0.024255881,0.006566162,-0.03143723,0.05621204,-0.012060981,0.037999205,0.05517418,-0.013492229,-0.07706976,-0.007239937,0.027838185,0.14262258,-0.01921722,0.11356238,-0.016254285,0.045197293,0.16070148,-0.022665605,-0.05564289,-0.10157673,-0.0023456558,-0.0034776807,0.027302513,-0.019116782,-0.075060986,-0.118584305,0.013952572,-0.053399764,0.03332882,0.05319889,0.21413474,-0.043724198,-0.037965726,0.0755297,-0.04144759,0.08771623,0.054772425,-0.025277006,0.013852133,0.03152093,-0.010654843,-0.12842727,0.12132963,0.052027106,0.05242886,0.036057398,0.059995223,0.026013553,0.09749224,0.0020924674,0.012822639,0.011617378,0.02877561,0.03582304,0.14342608,-0.014873258,-0.046369076,0.11650858,0.03786529,-0.16471903,-0.15614828,0.0564464,-0.043958552,0.08664489,0.04874612,-0.026113993,0.041213237,-0.047574338,-0.032223996,-0.0073403753,0.0006408181,-0.009457952,0.0055994424,0.019384617,-0.009424473,0.073989645,0.026499007,0.021376645,0.050118778,0.022314072,0.038166605,-0.012655242,0.18346754,0.021945797,0.04017537,-0.029009966,0.09206856,-0.11496852,-0.14998806,0.11222321,-0.0016823437,0.06464887,0.05262974,-0.06625588,-0.045799926,0.043791156,0.0013632424,-0.028005583,0.009625349,-0.06160224,-0.009123158,0.034132328,0.001786967,0.0046954965,-0.02757035,-0.026365088,0.047641296,0.026783582,-0.02492547,-0.037363097,0.09963492,-0.019334398,0.07640017,-0.0680303,-0.03282663,0.026917499,-0.010085693,0.02298366,-0.068900764,-0.09655481,0.04285373,-0.012387406,-0.012270228,0.02048944,-0.07385573,0.045833405,-0.0740566,0.052161027,-0.07613233,-0.018848946,0.029964132,-0.04218414,0.1215305,0.0077756085 Because <strong>the light can travel 225000 km/s in water</strong>....\"},{\"title\":\"National Ocean Service - How far does light travel in the ocean? Sunlight entering the water may travel about 1,000 meters (3,280 feet) into the ocean under the right conditions, but there is rarely any significant light beyond 200 meters (656 feet). The ocean is divided into three zones based on depth and light level. The upper 200 meters (656 feet) of the ocean is called the euphotic, or \\\"sunlight,\\\" zone. This zone contains the vast majority of commercial fisheries and is home to many protected marine mam\",\"link\":\"https://m.facebook.com/usoceangov/photos/how-far-does-light-travel-in-the-oceansunlight-entering-the-water-may-travel-abo/10157466355137867/\",\"snippet\":\"How far does light travel in the ocean? <strong>Sunlight</strong> entering the <strong>water</strong> may travel about 1,000 meters (3,280 feet) into the ocean under the right...\"},{\"title\":\"How far does light travel in the ocean?\",\"link\":\"https://oceanservice.noaa.gov/facts/light_travel.html\",\"snippet\":\"<strong>Sunlight</strong> does not <strong>penetrate</strong> to these depths and the zone is bathed in darkness. The aphotic zone is further subdivided into the bathypelagic zone (or midnight zone) between 1,000 and 4,000 meters, the abyssopelagic (or the abyss) between 4,000 and 6,000 meters, and the hadopelagic zone (or hadal zone) 6,000 meters and deeper...\"},{\"title\":\"How far does light travel in the ocean? : Ocean Exploration Facts: NOAA Office of Ocean Exploration and Research\",\"link\":\"https://oceanexplorer.noaa.gov/facts/light-distributed.html\",\"snippet\":\"<strong>Sunlight does not penetrate the eternal darkness below 1,000 meters (3,280 feet),</strong> an area known as the aphotic zone, which includes the midnight zone (or bathypelagic zone) between 1,000 and 4,000 meters (3,280 and 13,123 feet), the abyss (or abyssopelagic zone) between 4,000 and 6,000 meters ...\"},{\"title\":\"Dispatches from the Deep: Light and Dark in the Sea | AMNH\",\"link\":\"https://www.amnh.org/learn-teach/curriculum-collections/deep-sea-vents/light-and-dark-in-the-sea\",\"snippet\":\"The ocean is very, very deep; <strong>light can only penetrate so far below the surface of the ocean</strong>. As the light energy travels through the water, the molecules in the water scatter and absorb it. At great depths, light is so scattered that there is nothing left to detect.\"},{\"title\":\"At what depth does sunlight not enter into the sea? - Quora\",\"link\":\"https://www.quora.com/At-what-depth-does-sunlight-not-enter-into-the-sea\",\"snippet\":\"Answer (1 of 9): The depth of the ocean is classified into following zones [1] based on the reach of the <strong>sunlight</strong> below sea-level. # Upto 200metres below the surface of the ocean is called the euphotic, or &quot;<strong>sunlight</strong>,&quot; zone. This zone contains the vast majority of commercial fisheries and is home ...\"},{\"title\":\"Descent Into Light\",\"link\":\"https://www.amnh.org/explore/descent-into-light\",\"snippet\":\"<strong>By 950 meters—around the deepest the submersible can go—only a few photons of light penetrate the ocean</strong>. For each deep dive, Sparks, Gruber, and Pieribone landed the vehicle gently on a mud-covered barren-looking bottom. The descent into light finishes here.\"},{\"title\":\"How Deep Light Penetrates the Ocean - American Oceans\",\"link\":\"https://www.americanoceans.org/facts/how-deep-does-light-penetrate-the-ocean/\",\"snippet\":\"<strong>Sunlight can penetrate up to 200 meters (656 feet) in clear ocean water</strong>, but in most parts of the ocean, the depth of penetration is much less. UV light can penetrate even deeper, but it is harmful to most marine organisms and is absorbed by the water before it reaches the ocean floor.\"},{\"title\":\"What is the deepest any amount of sunlight can reach in the ocean? - Quora\",\"link\":\"https://www.quora.com/What-is-the-deepest-any-amount-of-sunlight-can-reach-in-the-ocean\",\"snippet\":\"Answer (1 of 5): It is usually accepted that 200 metres is the extent of how far <strong>sunlight</strong> <strong>can</strong> travel below the surface. At this level everything lives in the dark. Some creatures down there have adapted to life in these low level light and some even produce there own headlamps. Because water strongly absorbs light, <strong>sunlight penetrates only to depths of about 200 meters (656 feet).</strong>\"},{\"title\":\"Light penetration in water - Responsible Seafood Advocate\",\"link\":\"https://www.globalseafood.org/advocate/light-penetration-in-water/\",\"snippet\":\"Light <strong>penetrating</strong> <strong>water</strong> is scattered and absorbed exponentially as it passes downward. The presence of dissolved organic matter and suspended solids further impedes light <strong>penetration</strong>, and different types of solids absorb different wavelengths.\"},{\"title\":\"How far can sunlight extend into the sea? - Quora\",\"link\":\"https://www.quora.com/How-far-can-sunlight-extend-into-the-sea\",\"snippet\":\"Answer (1 of 8): How far does light travel in the ocean? This experiment helps to demonstrate light reflection and absorption using objects of various colors. When <strong>sunlight</strong> strikes the ocean, some of it reflects off the surface back into the atmosphere. The amount of energy that <strong>penetrates</strong> the surface of the <strong>water</strong> ...\"},{\"title\":\"For how long can sunlight penetrate into a sea? - Quora\",\"link\":\"https://www.quora.com/For-how-long-can-sunlight-penetrate-into-a-sea\",\"snippet\":\"Quora is a place to gain and share knowledge. sea"],"similarityValue":0,"id":8,"type":"forward","messages":[{"role":"assistant","content":"Sunlight entering the water may travel about 1,000 meters (3,280 feet) into the ocean under the right conditions, but there is rarely any significant light beyond 200 meters (656 feet). The ocean is divided into three zones based on depth and light level: the upper 200 meters (656 feet) is called the euphotic or \"sunlight\" zone.\n\nGiven this information, it is unlikely that sunlight can reach the deepest part of the Black Sea, which has a maximum depth of 2,212 meters (7,257 feet). The depth and properties of the Black Sea would likely prevent significant sunlight penetration to its deepest
